steps.title: Identify what the x and y axes represent
steps.content: From the scatterplot, the x-axis is labeled Years since 1940, meaning $x$ is measured in individual years (0, 10, 20, ..., 70). The y-axis shows Minimum wage (dollars per hour). The line of best fit is $y = 0.096x - 0.488$, where the slope is $m = 0.096$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope as a rate of change
steps.content:
The slope represents $$\frac{\text{change in wage}}{\text{change in years}} = 0.096 \text{ dollars per year}$$
Since $x$ is measured in years, this slope directly tells us the average increase in minimum wage per year.

The line predicts an average increase of 0.096 dollars each year between 1940 and 2010.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
